name: What to Do with the Egg Yolks Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Butter a 10-inch tube pan and set aside.
2. In a large mixing bowl, combine the yeast, 1 teaspoon sugar, and water. Set aside for 10 minutes.
3. Combine the milk, butter, oil, zest, remaining sugar, and salt in the yeast mixture. Stir into the yeast mixture.
4. Add the egg yolks to the yeast mixture and stir well.
5. Gradually add the flour (1/2 cup at a time) to the mixture, stirring well after each addition to incorporate thoroughly.
6. Knead the dough for 5 to 10 minutes until it is smooth, elastic, and satiny.
7. Add the cranberries and pecans to the dough. Place the dough back into the bowl, cover, and let rise at room temperature until doubled in bulk.
8. Using a wooden spoon, beat down the risen dough for about a minute.
9. Transfer the dough to the buttered tube pan and allow it to rise at room temperature until doubled in bulk.
10. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
11. Bake the bread for 45 to 50 minutes or until it is a dark golden brown and sounds hollow when tapped.
12. Place on a rack to cool, or serve warm. Once cooled, the bread is also excellent sliced and toasted.
